428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g

Re
Anita Razo
Associate Governmental Program Analyst
Department of Health Care Services
3054 Montview Way
Sacramento, CA 95833
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-216
Dear Ms. Razo:
This letter resPonds to your request for advice regarding the revolving door
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 ffrii letter should not bJconstrued
as assistance on any conduct that may have already taken place. (See Regulation
18329(bX8XA).) In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. ihe Fair
Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when
it renders assistance. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Because your questions
are general in nature, we are treating your request as one for informat uriirtur"".i--.-""
Please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We
therefore offer no opinion on the application, if iny, of other conflict-of-interest laws
such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090. In addition,
we offer no opinion on the post-government employment restrictions of public Contract
Code Section 10411' You may wish to consult ihe Attorn"y General's office regarding
these provisions.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationl of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections I8ll0 through 18997 of ritle 2 of the california
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division'6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section g3l l4; Regulation lgjZq(c)t:), copy enclosedj


,File No. I-08-216
Page No. 2
QUESTTON
Should you accept employment with a private consulting firm, will the Act,s
revolving door prohibitions restrict appearances before or corlmunications with your
former govemmental agency employii, your involvement in other proceedings in which
you participated as a governmental employee, or your activities before state liealth
agencies not within the state of California?
CONCLUSION
Should you accept employment with the private employer, the Act,s one-year ban
prohibits you from appearing before or communicating witrr your former agency
gmpl-ov9r for compensation and for the purpose of infl-uencing administrative or
legislative action, orany action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment,
awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase of
goods or property. Moreover, the Act's-p_ermanent ban prohibits you rro* pirti"ipating
in any proceeding involving the State of 
-califomia 
or assistirg oth.r, in trr" pio.eeding if
the proceeding is one in which you participated while employed by the state.
FACTS
You work at the Department of Health care services (the ..DHCS,,) as an
Associate Govemmental program Analyst r ("AGPA',). rn this position, you conouct
onsite facility inspections/surveys in hospitals and medical faciliiies p"nuinlrrg ro
Subacute care Units' The inspections u-p trre primary function of the unit for"which you
work and are conducted to verify compliance with the Medi-cal Subacute Services
Contract and Title 22 Subacute Regulations. These inspections help to ia"n,iiv *y
issues relating to the facilities and illow you to recommend necessary,..rn"aij1 actions
or, in cases of severe non-compliance, penalties as mandated by the subacute regutations.
Your current duties include yearly facility inspections/surveys that vary from
standard facility revie.ws-to.making approval recommendations for new contracts,
increases in subacute beds in a, eiistirg contract, and facility closures. you also verify
complaints submitted to your unit, which are directly related to subacute care. Additional
office duties include serving as the lead analyst in your unit, rewriting or updating
subacute regulations, assisting in Assembly niu rewrites, training incoming staff, acting
as first line responder to any inquiries regarding subacute care, assessing unit processes
and procedures, and maintaining contract related data.
Recently, you have been offered-an opportunity with a private consulting firm
upon leaving the DHCS' If you accept ttris pisition, ytu, dutiei would include training
administrative staff and nursing staff, makir,g .."o--endations to assist facilities in
passing inspections/surveys by the DHCS, pioviding consulting services to assist
facilities in approvals for new applications/contracts and bed iricreases, "rrirrirg facilities
to correct issues related to non-compliance of their contracts, advising racility -


File No. I-08-216
Page No. 3
administrators, and marketing. Additionally-, y9u anticipate contacts with employees of
the DHCS during onsite visits by DHCS toiu"iliti"r thai contract with the private
consulting firm.
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types of post-
governmental employment provisions under the Act, colloquially k o*n u, tt.
"tt.v9tyi18 door" prohibitions. In addition, Section 87407 prohibits certain state and local
officials from making, participating in making, or using their official position to influence
decisions affecting persons with whom frgv are negotilting employment, or have any
:urangement conceming employment. (Also see Regulatiin tsi+i.)3
o One-Year Ban: The "one-year ban" prohibits a former state employee from
appearing before or communicating with, for compensation, his or her former
agency for the purpose of influencing any adminisirative or legislative actions or
any discretrg$, act involving the isiuance, amendment, u*ulairj, or rerocation
of a permit,_license, grant, or contract, or the sale or purchase or gJoas o,
properry. (See Section g7 406; Regulation lg7 46.1.)
The one-ye-.bT applies to any employee of a state administrative agency who
holds a position that is designated or should^ be designatedin the. agency,, 
"o"nfli.t-of-
interest code. (Section 87406(d)(l); Regulation ni+aJ(a)izll;Tr,ri"" "rri,., for t2
months from the date the employee Ieaves state office or r-pioy-ent, wtrict'is o"rirrea
as thedate the employee_permanently leaves state service or takes a leave of absence.
(Regulation 187 46.1(b)( I ) and (2).)
While in effect, the one-year ban applies only when a former employee or official
is being compensat"g f9^t h-is or her appearances or communications before his or her
former agency on behalf of any p".tori as an agent, attorney, or representative of that
person. (Regulation 18746.1(b)(3) and (a).)
In contrast to the permanent ban,.which only applies to'Judicial or quasi-judicial,,
proceedings, the one-year ban applies to "any upp"ur*.. or communication made for the
purpose of influencing administrative or tegislaiive action, or influencing *y u"tion or
proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation-of a permit,
According to the facts you have provided, you have already received an offer to work for a
private consulting firm' As yo1 ale currentiy discussing ttris otrerlor'employment wirh the private
consulting firm, you are "negotiating employment" witf, u p.orp""tlr. emptoyer while employed by the
DHCS' (Regulation 18747(c)(l)') eny iavice.issued ar il,ir ii-i" p..t rning to Section g7407 woutd retate
to past conduct' As the commission will not advise with respect to purt conduct, we provide no opinion as
to the application of SectiongT4oT to your particular.i..uritunr"r.' -'
a A govemmental employee should be designated in his or her agency's conflict-of-interest code
if the employee makes or participates in making governmental decisions that have a reasonably foreseeable
material effect on any financial interest. (Sectiin g7302.)


File No. I-08-216
Page No.4
license, grant, or contract, or the sare or purchase of goods or property.,, (Regulation
18746'l(b)(5).) An appearance or communication is for the "pu.po., or irnu"r"ing,, if it
is made for the "principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifying,
opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding." (Regulation 1g746.2.) An
appearance or communication includes, but is not limited to, conu"rsing by telephone or
in person, corresponding in writing or by electronic transmission, utt"nf,irri a meeting,
and delivering or sending any communiiation. (/d.)
Finally, appearances and communications are prohibited only if they are before a
state agency that the public official worked foror represented or a siate ug"r"y ..which
budget, personnel,-and other operations" are subject to the control of a state agency the
public official worked for or represented. (Regulation lg746.l(bX6).) 
----- -'
However, not all communications are prohibited by the one-year ban.
Appearances or communications before a former s-t1te agency employer, made as part of
"services performed to administer, implement, or fulfill ihe ,equiremlnts of an existing
permit, license, grant, contract, or sale agreement may be excluded from the [one-year]
prohibitions . . . provided the services do not involveihe issuance, amendment, awarding,
or revocation of any of these actions or proceedings." (Regulation 1g7*.rc)(5XA);
Quiring Advice Letter,No. 4-03-272; HananAdvice Letter, No. I_00_209.)
Additionally, Regulat ion rg7 46.2(bx l )-(4) provides rhat appearances or
communications are not restricted under the one-year ban, if an individual:
"(l) Participates as a panelist or formal speaker at a conference or similar
public event for educational purposes or to disseminate research and the
subject matter does not pertain to a specific action or proceeding;
"(2) Attends a general informational meeting, seminar, or similar event;
"(3) Requests information concerning any matter of public record; or
"(4) Communicates with the press."
We have also advised that a former agency official may, without violating the
one-year ban, draft proposals on a client's behalf to be submitied to the ug.*y'ro long as
the former employee is not identified in connection with the client,s efforts to influence
administrative action. (CookAdvice Letter, No. 4-95-3 2li Harrison Advice Letter, No.
A-92-289') Similarly, a-former agency official -uy ur" h-i, or her expertise to advise
clients on the procedural requirements, plans, o, pii.im oi trr. official,s former agency
so long as the employee is not identified with the employer's efforts to influence the
agency. (Perry Advice Letter, No. 4_94_004.)
From the facts presented, you make or participate in making govemmentar
decisions in your position as an AGPA I with itre prics that have?e"as";;i;f;r.seeable

material effects on financial interests. Although you have not indicated whether this
position is a.designated position in DHcS's cJnflict-or-interest code, your facts indicate
that this position should be a designated position. (See iections g7300-g7313.)
Accordingly, should you elect to Gave ttre oucs to work for the private employer, your
activities as a former state employee will be restricted under the one-year uui. you t uu.

communication with the DHCS in representation of youi anticipated private employer.
Hopefully, this assistance will help you in making ruiurc determinations or wnether any
particular appearance or communication is prohib"ited.
At this time, we can only offer general observations relating to the application of
the one-year ban to some of the duties,you anticipate performing for the private employer
upon leaving your position with the oHcs. e"tilipuiJ duties lou rrauJoescribed
'^"lldt training administrative staff and nursing starf mating recommendations to assist
facilities in passing inspections/surveys by the bHcs, pr"viding consulting services to
assist facilities in approvals for new applitationy"ontru"t, and bed increasfs, assisting
facilities to correct issues related to non-compliance, advising facility administrators, and
marketing' As noted above, the one-year.ban-would'not prohibit you from performing
duties on behalf of the priv^ate employer that do not involve an appearance before or
communication with your former governmental employer. (Reguration l g746. I (b)(5).)
Accordingly, you may perfo^rm the types of duties drr"riura ub-ou" so tong u, it.r" duties
are done behind the scenes for DHC$and the do not involve a proceeding that would be
subject to the restrictions of the permanent ban. (Cook, iupro; Harrtson, supra; and
Perry, supra.)s
Potentially troublesome under the one-year ban, you also anticipate contact with

visits to facilities that contract wittr-theprivate employer-without the factual context of
specific contacts, however, we cannot ditermine whether these contacts involve the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of u p"a-it, ticense, grant, or contract.
while you may perform servicis "to administ.., i-pr.-"nr, o, fulfill the requirements of
an existing permit, license, grant, contract, or sale alreement,, under Regulation
r8746'l(b)(5XA), this exception is n:urow and does not apply if the services..involve the
issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation" of u p"a-ir, license, grant, or contract.
For example, this exception wouto allow a contact with a DHCS employee to ask for a
determination of a facility's compliance with an "rirtirj.ontract. However, this
exception would not permit you !o contact, DIqg "*frloy." to advocate rhat a facility,s
efforts comply with contractual terms when a finding oin-Jnco-ptiance by the DHCS
would subject the facility to the revocation of a penriit, .orriru",, or license. If you need
further assistance relatingto a specific appearance or communication, you should seek
further advice providing all relevant facts.

former agency employer' The one-year ban does not riitrict appearances or communications, in
representation of the private employer, before or with state hjih rg.i"i"r not within rhe state of carifornia
so long as the appearance or communication is not arso before or with the DHCS.

o Permanent Ban-: The "permanent ban" prohibits a former state employee from
"switching sides" and participating, for compensation, in any ,p".if;" proceeding
involving the State of California oi assisting others in the p.o"r"aing ir rrr"
proceeding is one in which the former state-employee participated wiile
employed by the stare. (See Sections g74u_g;402; Regulation lg74l.l.)
The permanent ban is a lifetime bal and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or
olLel qroceeding in which you participatea wnite you served as a state administrative
official. "'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other pro"."iin!'--"*, any proceeding,
application, request for a ruling or other deierminatiJn, .ont u"t, tlaim, controversy,

party or parties in any court or state administrative lgency . . ..,, (Sectlon iz+oo(c).)

investigation, or use of confidential informutio, . . ..; ?s""tion g7a00(d).)
"The permanent ban does not apply to a 'new' proceeding even in cases where the
new proceeding is related to or grows out tf a prior proceeding in which the official had
participated' A 'new'-proceeding not subject to trr. iermanent ban typically involves
different parties, adifferent subject matter, or different factual issues from those
considered in previous proceedings." (Rrs/Advice r-etter, No. A-04-rg7; arso see

agency in which the former employee did not participate are considered new proceedings.
(Leslie Advice Letter, No. I-g9-64q.1 a new ctntra"i i, or" that is based on new

employer.

(See Regulation 18329(bX8XA).) In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. ihe Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Because your questions are general in nature, we are treating your request as one for informat uriirtur"".i--.-"" Please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if iny, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090. In addition, we offer no opinion on the post-government employment restrictions of public Contract Code Section 10411' You may wish to consult ihe Attorn"y General's office regarding these provisions. ' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated.
